perturbation.original_text: DeltaThree shall reimburse PrimeCall for all costs incurred by PrimeCall in connection with the manufacturing and printing of the Calling Cards.
perturbation.changed_text: DeltaThree may, at its discretion, reimburse PrimeCall for costs associated with the manufacturing and printing of the Calling Cards, contingent upon budget availability.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates reimbursement, creating a contractual obligation. The changed text introduces 'may, at its discretion' and 'contingent upon budget availability', turning it into a discretionary action based on DeltaThree's internal budgeting. This contradicts the original obligation. This pertubation creates an in-text contradiction with Article V [Payments] which refers to payments being calculated on a monthly basis and expected without disgreationary discretion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE V [Payments] Payments Section 5.01. Payment Terms. Any amounts due hereunder shall be calculated and paid in U.S. dollars on a monthly basis within twenty-five (25) business days following the receipt of the reports detailed in Sections 3.06 [Reporting] and 4.02 [Reporting] hereof or any other invoices provided for herein. All payments shall be made via wire transfer in accordance with written instructions from the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated reimbursement for costs. The changed text makes reimbursement discretionary ('may, at its discretion, contingent upon budget availability'). Article V states that payments are calculated and paid on a monthly basis without discretionary assessment. Thus, the discretionary language in the changed text directly contradicts the mandatory payment terms outlined in Article V, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: PrimeCall agrees to cooperate and provide necessary support to assist DeltaThree in providing the services under this Section 4.01 [Web Site Development and Services] and shall be responsible for supplying DeltaThree with the content and graphics (look and feel) of the PrimeCall website.
perturbation.changed_text: PrimeCall will endeavor to cooperate and provide support, as reasonably feasible, to assist DeltaThree in providing the services under this Section 4.01 [Web Site Development and Services], and should consider supplying DeltaThree with the content and graphics (look and feel) of the PrimeCall website.
perturbation.explanation: The original text uses definitive language ('agrees to cooperate', 'shall be responsible'). The changed text uses weaker language ('will endeavor to cooperate', 'as reasonably feasible', 'should consider'). This weakens PrimeCall's obligations, introducing ambiguity. This pertubation creates an in-text contradiction since DeltaThree is required to establish and administrate a PrimeCall web site, yet the original term has been modified to only 'endeavor' or 'should consider'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 4.01 [Web Site Development and Services]. Web Site Development and Services. Within three (3) months of the date hereof, DeltaThree shall establish and administrate a PrimeCall web site (the "PrimeCall Web Site") in consultation with PrimeCall which will enable PrimeCall to market and sell its calling cards via the World Wide Web and provide on-line support for such sales. The PrimeCall Web Site shall be a fully functional e-commerce site, with all ordering and billing performed on-line. DeltaThree shall provide ongoing upgrades to the PrimeCall Web Site, shall develop a database for the PrimeCall Web Site, and shall oversee all billing, collections and fulfillment for on-line orders. PrimeCall shall establish its own merchant account with Citibank or another accredited United States banking institution and provide DeltaThree with all information pertaining to such account to enable DeltaThree to process all on-line credit card transactions for PrimeCall. PrimeCall shall be fully liable for all charge-backs, refunds and commission payments associated with the processing of PrimeCall's on-line credit card transactions by DeltaThree. DeltaThree shall establish for PrimeCall an on-line interactive center whereby PrimeCall shall be able to monitor the on-line sales of its calling cards. All calling card and pricing information shall be provided by PrimeCall. DeltaThree shall be responsible for all costs and expenses in connection with the operation and maintenance of the PrimeCall Web Site and the services provided pursuant to this Section 4.01 [Web Site Development and Services] shall be provided at no additional cost to PrimeCall; provided, however, that PrimeCall shall be fully responsible for all costs associated with the purchase of hardware, software and domain names related to the PrimeCall website.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated PrimeCall 'shall be responsible' for providing content, while the changed text weakens this to 'should consider'. The contradicted text requires DeltaThree to establish and administrate the website, based on PrimeCall providing information. If PrimeCall only 'should consider' providing content, then DeltaThree's ability to fulfill its obligation is compromised, creating a direct conflict and making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology definition, as the change introduces ambiguity where certainty was expected.

perturbation.original_text: PrimeCall and DeltaThree each agree that for the longest period permitted by law each shall hold in strictest confidence and, without the prior written approval of the other party hereto, not to use for their own benefit or the benefit of any party other than the other party hereto, or disclose to any person, firm or corporation other than such party (other than as required by law) any confidential proprietary information concerning the business and affairs of the other party hereto
perturbation.changed_text: PrimeCall and DeltaThree may consider, subject to internal policies, holding certain information confidential, and may, unless explicitly prohibited by internal guidelines, share business and affairs data with affiliated entities, unless deemed detrimental to ongoing negotiations.
perturbation.explanation: The original text imposes a strict confidentiality obligation. The changed text makes confidentiality discretionary ('may consider') and allows for disclosure to affiliated entities unless detrimental to negotiations, severely weakening the confidentiality provision. The pertubation directly contradicts the Article VIII [Confidentiality] and the Equitable remedies provided for a beach of confidentiality are impossible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 8.02 [Confidentiality]. Equitable Remedies PrimeCall and DeltaThree each specifically recognize that any breach of Section 8.01 [Confidentiality] will cause irreparable injury to the other party hereto and that actual damages may be difficult to ascertain, and in any event, may be inadequate. Accordingly (and without limiting the availability of legal or equitable, including injunctive, remedies under any other provisions of this Agreement), each of PrimeCall and DeltaThree agrees that in the event of any such breach, the other party hereto shall be entitled to injunctive relief in addition to such other legal and equitable remedies that may be available. In addition, PrimeCall and DeltaThree each agree that the provisions of Section 8.01 [Confidentiality] shall be considered separate and apart from the remaining provisions of this Agreement and shall be enforced as such.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the confidentiality obligations compared to the original text. The original text mandated strict confidentiality, while the changed text makes it discretionary and allows disclosure to affiliates. This directly contradicts Section 8.02, which states that any breach of confidentiality will cause irreparable injury and entitles the other party to injunctive relief. The discretionary nature of confidentiality in the changed text makes it impossible to guarantee confidentiality, directly conflicting with the irreparable injury clause and making equitable remedies unenforceable.
